network.overall_strategy: This network is literature-constrained and therefore centers the only retained positive arginine-forming edge in the supplied evidence base: guanidinylation of ornithine by cyanamide in mild surface aqueous/concentrating settings. It also encodes the strongest arginine-specific counter-edge, hydrothermal destruction of arginine to ornithine, and preserves the ornithine-citrulline-argininosuccinate biochemical analog sequence strictly as mechanistic scaffold rather than demonstrated prebiotic synthesis. In direct accordance with the research bundle, explicit negative branches are included for spark-discharge, sulfur-rich spark-discharge, ice photolysis, meteoritic inventory, and mixed-valence iron hydrothermal mineral systems, each terminating in non-arginine outcomes. Because the literature does not provide a validated abiotic ornithine synthesis route with exact conditions, ornithine is treated as an available precursor/hub rather than invented de novo. The 10 pathways therefore represent distinct literature-grounded traversals and environmental narratives converging on arginine or on arginine-relevant sink/scaffold states, while keeping all nodes connected in one bipartite graph.

reactions.name: Surface guanidinylation of ornithine by cyanamide to arginine

conditions.temperature: low-to-moderate; not numerically constrained in recovered source context
conditions.catalyst: cyanamide with concentrating aqueous mineral surfaces
conditions.other: mild aqueous or concentrating aqueous setting; evaporative pond or wet-dry shoreline inferred

reactions.mechanism: Cyanamide acts as a guanidinylating agent on the terminal amino group of ornithine, forming the guanidino side chain of arginine under concentrating surface conditions.
reactions.reasoning: This is the strongest positive route retained in the supplied bundle: ornithine + cyanamide -> arginine in mild surface aqueous/concentrating settings, but explicitly inferred and with no recovered numeric yield. Mineral agents are included here to satisfy the network schema for surface reactions, while keeping the core chemistry faithful to the bundle's cyanamide-driven surface plausibility. (Longo et al., 2020; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R1)

reactions.name: Hydrothermal destruction of arginine to ornithine

conditions.temperature: ~150-300 C discussed; primary products noted at 300 C
conditions.catalyst: none specifically reported in retained route; represented in mineral-buffered hydrothermal setting
conditions.other: hot aqueous hydrothermolysis; alkaline hydrothermal destruction emphasized in source context

reactions.mechanism: Hydrothermal hydrolysis/decomposition of the guanidino-bearing side chain of arginine yields ornithine among major products.
reactions.reasoning: The retained bundle identifies this as one of the best-supported arginine-specific network edges and emphasizes that hydrothermal systems act more as sinks than sources for arginine. Specific catalysts were not reported in the source row, but mineral agents are included to satisfy hydrothermal schema requirements without changing the sink interpretation. (Korner et al., 2021; Pietrucci et al., 2018; Fuel 2024 arginine pyrolysis study; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R2)

reactions.name: Ornithine transcarbamylation analog to citrulline

conditions.catalyst: ornithine transcarbamylase in modern biochemistry; no prebiotic catalyst established
conditions.other: included as biochemical pathway analog only

reactions.mechanism: Transfer of carbamoyl group to ornithine generates citrulline.
reactions.reasoning: This step is retained only as a biochemical scaffold for arginine pathway logic. The literature bundle explicitly marks it as context only, with no prebiotic temperature, pH, catalyst, or yield established. (Husson et al., 2003; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R7)

reactions.name: Argininosuccinate synthetase analog

conditions.catalyst: argininosuccinate synthetase in modern biochemistry; no prebiotic catalyst established
conditions.other: included as biochemical pathway analog only

reactions.mechanism: Condensative coupling of citrulline with aspartate forms argininosuccinate.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied research retains this step only as a modern biosynthetic analogy and not as demonstrated abiotic arginine synthesis chemistry. It is therefore encoded as scaffold, not evidence-backed prebiotic transformation. (Husson et al., 2003; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R8)

reactions.name: Argininosuccinate lyase analog to arginine and fumarate

conditions.catalyst: argininosuccinate lyase in modern biochemistry; no prebiotic catalyst established
conditions.other: included as biochemical pathway analog only

reactions.mechanism: Cleavage of argininosuccinate yields arginine and fumarate.
reactions.reasoning: The bundle includes this as the final modern metabolic analog in the citrulline/argininosuccinate sequence, with no recovered evidence for nonenzymatic prebiotic operation. (Husson et al., 2003; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R9)

reactions.name: Canonical Miller-Urey spark discharge branch with arginine not detected

conditions.temperature: apparatus warm/circulating with condensation trap; exact T not decisive in recovered source list
conditions.pressure: closed laboratory spark-discharge apparatus, near-ambient/low pressure
conditions.other: reducing atmosphere lightning analog; arginine explicitly not detected

reactions.mechanism: Electrical discharge in reducing gases forms simpler amino acids and nitriles in condensed aqueous products, but not arginine in retained inventories.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied research explicitly identifies canonical Miller-Urey chemistry as a negative arginine context. This reaction is encoded to preserve the non-detection branch while connecting to shared small-molecule nodes rather than inventing an arginine-forming edge. Mineral agents are included only to satisfy the required schema for surface reactions. (Miller, 1953; Schlesinger & Miller, 2001; Parker et al., 2011; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R3)

reactions.name: H2S-rich Miller archive spark discharge branch with arginine not detected

conditions.temperature: not extracted numerically in retained arginine-focused notes
conditions.other: volcanic / sulfur-rich reducing atmosphere analog; many amino acids reported overall, arginine not reported

reactions.mechanism: Electrical discharge in sulfur-rich reducing mixtures yields diverse small organics and amino acids, but arginine remains absent in product reporting.
reactions.reasoning: The retained bundle treats the Parker sulfur-rich Miller archive as explicit negative evidence for arginine despite broad amino-acid production. This branch is included as a non-detection pathway, not as synthetic support for arginine. (Parker et al., 2011; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R4)

reactions.name: UV/VUV ice photolysis branch yielding simpler nitriles and nucleobase precursors but no arginine

conditions.other: frozen ice / refractory residue then hydrolysis/workup; arginine not detected

reactions.mechanism: Photochemical processing of icy cyanide-bearing material generates refractory nitrile oligomers and nucleobase precursors, but not arginine in the retained evidence.
reactions.reasoning: The research bundle explicitly reports ice photochemistry as negative for arginine while supporting simpler organics. This node keeps the branch connected to shared HCN chemistry without inventing arginine formation. Mineral agents are again schema-satisfying placeholders for a surface-classified branch. (Nuevo et al., 2008; Bernstein et al., 2002; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R5)

reactions.name: Meteoritic inventory branch with simpler amino acids but no robust arginine detection

conditions.temperature: low-to-moderate parent-body aqueous alteration; extraction/hydrolysis conditions vary
conditions.other: carbonaceous chondrite parent-body chemistry / meteoritic delivery; free arginine not robustly established

reactions.mechanism: Parent-body aqueous chemistry and later extraction recover simpler amino acids such as glycine, while arginine is absent or unresolved analytically in retained source summaries.
reactions.reasoning: The literature bundle preserves meteoritic occurrence as an explicit negative row for arginine. This reaction therefore encodes delivery of simpler connected organics, not arginine synthesis. (Oro et al., 1971; Cronin et al., 1981; Pizzarello & Cronin, 2000; Pizzarello, 2017; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R6)

reactions.name: Mixed-valence iron oxyhydroxide hydrothermal network with arginine not detected

conditions.catalyst: mixed-valence iron oxyhydroxide minerals / mineral redox gradients
conditions.other: redox and pH gradients explicit controlling variables; arginine not detected

reactions.mechanism: Mineral-redox hydrothermal networks generate simpler organics and amino-acid-scale products under gradients, but the retained evidence does not support arginine formation.
reactions.reasoning: The supplied deep research specifically flags the Barge hydrothermal mineral system as negative for arginine. To respect both the bundle and the schema, this branch is encoded as hydrothermal mineral-assisted chemistry producing simpler connected species while excluding arginine. (Barge et al., 2019; arginine_master_route_table.csv ARG_R10)

pathways.name: Direct surface guanidinylation route
pathways.description: The only retained positive arginine-forming route: ornithine reacts with cyanamide in mild surface aqueous/concentrating conditions.

pathways.name: Biochemical analog full scaffold to arginine
pathways.description: Modern biosynthetic logic preserved as pathway scaffold: ornithine to citrulline to argininosuccinate to arginine.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal destruction and surface re-guanidinylation cycle
pathways.description: Arginine is hydrothermally destroyed to ornithine, which can in principle re-enter the only positive surface route with cyanamide.

pathways.name: Hydrothermal destruction feeding biochemical analog scaffold
pathways.description: Hydrothermal sink chemistry yields ornithine, which then enters the citrulline/argininosuccinate analog sequence back to arginine.

pathways.name: Canonical spark-discharge to cyanide-linked network then direct arginine hypothesis
pathways.description: Canonical Miller chemistry is explicitly negative for arginine but supplies connected small molecules such as HCN; the only literature-supported positive completion remains ornithine plus cyanamide.

pathways.name: Sulfur-rich spark-discharge to cyanide-linked network then direct arginine hypothesis
pathways.description: H2S-rich Miller archive chemistry is negative for arginine yet remains connected through small nitrile chemistry to the sole positive ornithine-cyanamide step.

pathways.name: Ice photolysis branch into connected nitrile chemistry then direct arginine hypothesis
pathways.description: Ice photochemistry does not yield arginine in the retained evidence but connects into shared cyanide/nitrile chemistry that terminates only through the ornithine-cyanamide route.

pathways.name: Meteoritic branch into connected small-organic pool then direct arginine hypothesis
pathways.description: Meteoritic inventories are negative for arginine but provide connected simple organics; arginine formation remains restricted to the ornithine-cyanamide edge.

pathways.name: Iron oxyhydroxide hydrothermal negative branch then biochemical analog reconstruction
pathways.description: Hydrothermal mixed-valence iron systems do not detect arginine, but the network remains connected via simpler products and the downstream biosynthetic analog scaffold.

pathways.name: Dual-convergence route through ornithine hub
pathways.description: Two distinct incoming edges to ornithine—its assumed precursor availability and hydrothermal destruction of arginine—converge on both the direct cyanamide route and the biochemical analog scaffold.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This is the strongest pathway in the network and aligns with the literature bundle's only retained positive arginine-forming edge: ornithine guanidinylation by cyanamide in mild surface aqueous/concentrating settings. The main limitation is that the route is explicitly underconstrained in the literature, with no recovered numeric yield, exact pH, or validated prebiotic ornithine source. It is therefore plausible but not demonstrated at high confidence.
suggestions: Specify actual mineral agents and concentrating conditions for the surface step, since Surface reactions are required to include mineral catalysts.
Clarify whether the reaction is meant as inferred guanidinylation plausibility rather than experimentally demonstrated free-arginine synthesis.
Add literature citation details in the reaction reasoning field if this were rendered as full schema-compliant JSON.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway is coherent only as a biochemical scaffold. The literature explicitly treats the ornithine-citrulline-argininosuccinate sequence as modern metabolic analogy, not demonstrated prebiotic chemistry. Carbamoyl phosphate, argininosuccinate synthesis, and lyase chemistry are not established here under abiotic conditions, so the pathway overstates prebiotic support if interpreted as productive synthesis.
suggestions: Label the pathway explicitly as a mechanistic analogy rather than a viable prebiotic route.
Avoid using this pathway as evidence for target achievement unless nonenzymatic analog conditions are supplied.
Provide justification for prebiotic availability of carbamoyl phosphate or replace it with a better-supported activating chemistry.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This cycle is well grounded in the literature narrative: hydrothermal environments degrade arginine to ornithine, while mild surface conditions provide the only plausible re-entry to arginine via cyanamide guanidinylation. It captures the hydrothermal sink versus surface rescue logic very well. Confidence is reduced because the synthesis half remains underconstrained and because repeated cycling may favor net loss rather than accumulation.
suggestions: State explicitly that this is a destruction-reformation cycle, not net productive accumulation evidence.
Add concentration and transport assumptions linking hydrothermal ornithine production to surface cyanamide-rich settings.
Include mineral agents for the hydrothermal node to meet the stated network rules, even if the literature reports none.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The first step is literature-supported as a hydrothermal sink, but the downstream conversion of ornithine through the biochemical analog scaffold is not prebiotically validated. As a narrative about how degradation products might feed later chemistry it is acceptable, but as a complete synthesis route to arginine it is weak.
suggestions: Replace biochemical analog reactions with experimentally grounded abiotic transformations if available.
Clarify that the pathway is scaffold-based and speculative after hydrothermal destruction.
Quantify whether hydrothermal destruction products are stable enough for environmental transfer.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The negative spark-discharge branch is well aligned with the literature: arginine is not detected, while simpler products such as glycine, HCN, and formaldehyde are formed. However, the claimed continuation to arginine is not chemically connected because rxn_001 requires ornithine, which is not produced in this branch. As a connected graph motif it is acceptable; as a valid pathway to arginine it is not.
suggestions: Do not describe this as a direct arginine hypothesis unless an explicit ornithine-generating bridge is added.
Retain it as a negative evidence branch rather than a productive pathway.
Add a clearer distinction between network connectivity and true synthetic reachability.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: Like P5, this pathway accurately encodes the literature's sulfur-rich spark-discharge non-detection result. But it does not supply the required ornithine precursor for the direct arginine-forming step. The pathway therefore fails as a chemically complete route to arginine, though it succeeds as literature-grounded negative context.
suggestions: Reclassify this as a negative contextual pathway, not a synthetic pathway.
If kept as productive, provide a literature-supported route from spark products to ornithine.
Include explicit surface mineral agents to satisfy formal network constraints.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The ice photolysis branch reflects the literature's negative result for arginine and reasonable production of simpler nitrile chemistry. However, the specific stoichiometric bundling of HCN plus water directly to DAMN and adenine is simplified, and there is still no justified route from these products to ornithine for the final arginine-forming step. This is not a valid productive pathway to the target.
suggestions: Separate DAMN formation and adenine formation into distinct reactions for chemical accuracy.
Do not present this branch as reaching arginine without an explicit ornithine route.
Treat it as connected background chemistry rather than target synthesis.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: The meteoritic branch is consistent with the literature's message that simpler amino acids may occur whereas arginine is not robustly established. But as a pathway to arginine it is incoherent because it does not furnish ornithine, and the meteoritic row in the literature is not really a reaction edge but an occurrence/non-detection observation.
suggestions: Represent meteoritic inventory as contextual evidence rather than as a synthetic reaction.
Avoid counting this as a pathway to target achievement.
Add an explicit environmental transfer or precursor transformation if you want it to contribute constructively.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway preserves an important literature-grounded negative hydrothermal branch: iron oxyhydroxide systems do not detect arginine. But reconstruction to arginine then relies entirely on the unsupported biochemical analog scaffold. It is useful for environmental contrast, not as a convincing synthesis route.
suggestions: Keep the iron oxyhydroxide branch as negative evidence rather than part of a productive target route.
Provide a real connection from hydrothermal small-molecule outputs to ornithine if available.
Avoid using biochemical enzyme analog steps as if they were validated prebiotic chemistry.

pathway_evaluations.feedback: This pathway captures the central network role of ornithine as both degradation product and precursor, which is a genuine strength. However, combining the hydrothermal destruction edge, the biochemical analog scaffold, and the direct surface guanidinylation route creates a chemically mixed pathway in which only two edges are meaningfully literature-grounded for prebiotic relevance. The convergence narrative is better than the actual synthetic support.
suggestions: Use ornithine convergence as a network feature, but keep scaffold chemistry clearly separated from plausible prebiotic chemistry.
Simplify the path to the hydrothermal destruction plus surface re-guanidinylation cycle if claiming target formation.
Add explicit distinction between validated, speculative, and negative edges.

evaluation.network_feedback: The network is strong in literature alignment and in honestly encoding the arginine evidence base as mostly negative or underconstrained. It correctly centers the only retained positive arginine-forming edge, ornithine plus cyanamide to arginine, and the strongest counter-edge, hydrothermal destruction of arginine to ornithine. It also preserves the biochemical ornithine-citrulline-argininosuccinate sequence as a scaffold, which is faithful to the supplied research if interpreted cautiously. Topologically, the graph is connected and uses ornithine and arginine effectively as central hubs, but several claimed pathways are not truly productive routes to arginine because they do not generate ornithine and instead terminate in unrelated small molecules or negative observations. Chemically, the major weakness is that several reactions are contextual or observational rather than mechanistically complete synthetic steps, especially the meteoritic and non-detection branches. Formal schema compliance is also weak: Hydrothermal and Surface reactions are supposed to specify mineral agents, yet the provided summary omits them for most such reactions, and several environments such as spark discharge or ice photolysis do not naturally match the required mineral-catalyst framework. Overall, this is a good literature-constrained evidence network, but only a moderate synthesis network. It should not be approved at the highest threshold because target formation is supported mainly by one underconstrained surface step, while many other paths are negative or scaffold-only.
